content_en: b) Subjugating Asad ad-Deen Shirkuh's Mamelukes to his authority, and taking full control of the troops after treating them well. c) Strengthening his position by means of the military help supplied to him by Noor ad-Deen Mahmood. His brother Shams ad-Dawlah Turanshah ibn Ayub arrived with some of these military reinforcements.612 The arrangements that Salah ad-Deen made led to him tightening his grip on the affairs of the state, and further weakened al-'Adid's authority, thus undermining the position of the caliph and disappointing Kabeer atTawashiyah (the chief eunuch), the caliph's confidant, who was a Nubian and commander of the Sudanese troops. He realized that Salah ad-Deen's policy in ruling, if it continued, was going to destroy the Fatimid state sooner or later, and it seems that he was one of those who had hoped to take over Shawar's position. When he did not succeed in doing so, he started plotting to topple Salah ad-Deen. He tried to make contact with Amalric I, King of Jerusalem, to incite him to attack Egypt in the hope that if he responded, Salah ad-Deen would go out to confront him, and he would be able to capture those of his companions whom he left behind in Cairo, gain the position of vizier and share the country with the Crusaders. But Salah ad-Deen learned of the conspiracy when one of his followers became suspicious about the shape of the shoes worn by the messenger of the caliph's confidant to Amalric I. He took the shoes, undid their stitching, and found a letter inside. So Salah ad-Deen arrested the caliph's confidant and took this opportunity to get rid of him. But the news that Salah ad-Deen's position in Egypt had been shaken encouraged the Christians to try again to attack the country.613 Salah ad-Deen fired all the Sudanese servants from the caliph's palace and appointed Baha' ad-Deen Qaraqoosh in charge of everyone in the palace, so that nothing happened in the palace, major or minor, without his command.614 The battle with the Sudanese troops When at-Tawashi,615 the caliph's confidant and Abyssinian servant, was killed and the other servants were dismissed, their Sudanese compatriots became angry, and nearly fifty thousand of them gathered and fought with the army of Salah ad-Deen, in between the two palaces. Large numbers on both sides were killed. Al-'Adid watched the battle from the palace, and the Syrian army was struck with stones and arrows coming from the direction of his palace. It was said that this happened on al-'Adid's orders, and it was said that this was not the case. Then Salah ad-Deen's brother, Shams al-Dawlah Turanshah — who had come to fight and had been sent by Noor ad-Deen to help and support his brother — ordered that al-'Adid's balcony be burned. Then the door was opened and a voice called out: Ameer al-Mu'mineen commands you to expel these Sudanese people from among you, and from your land. The morale of the Syrians was strengthened whilst the resolve of the Sudanese troops grew very weak. Salah ad-Deen sent troops to their place, which was known as al-Mansoorah, where they had their houses and families, near Bab Zuwaylah, and burned it. At that point they turned and fled, with swords hitting them from all directions, and many of them were killed. Then they asked Salah ad-Deen for protection and he granted it to them, and banished them to Giza. Then Shams al-Dawlah Turanshah, the brother of Salah ad-Deen, went out to them and killed most of them, and only a few of them were left. Those are their houses, desolate because of the wrong they had done (Quran 27: 52) It seems that the Fatimid ruler of Egypt at that time, al-'Adid, was aware of the conspiracy of the caliph's confidant, because it is impossible to imagine all of that happening in his palace without his knowledge. This is supported by the fact that as they were trying to eliminate the conspirators, Salah ad-Deen's troops were exposed to attacks with stones and arrows coming from the direction of the ruler's palace and, indeed, al-'Adid watched the battle from the palace.616 Discovery of the conspiracy was the responsibility of the intelligence department, and specifically of al-Qadi al-Fadil, who was the brains behind the demise of Fatimid influence in Egypt and the establishment of the Sunni madh-hab. We will discuss him in detail insha' Allah when we discuss the Ayubid state. Al-Qadi al-Fadil was watching the scribes of the intelligence department, especially those among them who had been laid off. Spies were everywhere, in the palaces, among the troops, along the border, at every stage along the roads, watching communications between Egypt and the Franks. These spies were in direct contact with al-Qadi alFadil, sending him reports via messengers and carrier pigeons.617 Dealing with the Armenians But Salah ad-Deen's victory did not stop with his putting an end to the Sudanese troops' influence; rather he followed that by putting an end to the Armenians' influence too. They were second to the Sudanese in strength and numbers. He burned the Armenian houses between the two palaces, where there was a large number of Armenian troops, most of whom were archers who had salaries from the government. They had tried to block Salah adDeen's forces during the battle with the Sudanese by shooting arrows, so they now got their just desserts.
content_id: b) Menaklukkan Mamluk Asad ad-Deen Shirkuh ke bawah otoritasnya, dan mengambil kendali penuh atas pasukan setelah memperlakukan mereka dengan baik. c) Memperkuat kedudukannya melalui bantuan militer yang disediakan oleh Noor ad-Deen Mahmood. Saudaranya Syamsuddin Turanshah ibn Ayyub tiba dengan sebagian bala bantuan militer ini. Langkah-langkah yang diambil Salahuddin menyebabkan dirinya semakin mencengkeram urusan negara, dan semakin melemahkan otoritas al-'Adid, sehingga merongrong kedudukan khalifah dan mengecewakan Kabir at-Tawasyiyah (kepala kasim), kepercayaan khalifah, yang merupakan orang Nubia dan komandan pasukan Sudan. Ia menyadari bahwa kebijakan Salahuddin dalam pemerintahan, jika terus berlanjut, akan menghancurkan negara Fathimiyyin cepat atau lambat, dan tampaknya ia adalah salah satu dari mereka yang berharap dapat mengambil alih posisi Shawar. Ketika ia gagal melakukannya, ia mulai merencanakan untuk menggulingkan Salahuddin. Ia mencoba menghubungi Amalric I, Raja Yerusalem, untuk menghasutnya menyerang Mesir dengan harapan bahwa jika ia merespons, Salahuddin akan keluar untuk menghadapinya, dan ia akan dapat menangkap rekan-rekan Salahuddin yang ditinggalkan di Kairo, merebut jabatan wazir, dan membagi negeri itu dengan Tentara Salib. Namun Salahuddin mengetahui konspirasi itu ketika salah seorang pengikutnya curiga dengan bentuk sepatu yang dipakai utusan kepercayaan khalifah kepada Amalric I. Ia mengambil sepatu itu, membuka jahitannya, dan menemukan surat di dalamnya. Maka Salahuddin pun menangkap kepercayaan khalifah dan memanfaatkan kesempatan ini untuk menyingkirkannya. Namun berita bahwa kedudukan Salahuddin di Mesir telah tergoncang mendorong kaum Nasrani untuk mencoba lagi menyerang negeri itu. Salahuddin memecat semua pelayan Sudan dari istana khalifah dan menunjuk Baha'uddin Qaraqosy untuk mengawasi semua orang di istana, sehingga tidak ada yang terjadi di istana, baik besar maupun kecil, tanpa seizinnya. Pertempuran dengan pasukan Sudan Ketika at-Tawasyi, kepercayaan khalifah dan pelayan Habasyah, dibunuh dan para pelayan lainnya dipecat, rekan-rekan Sudan mereka menjadi marah. Hampir lima puluh ribu dari mereka berkumpul dan bertempur melawan pasukan Salahuddin di antara dua istana. Jumlah besar dari kedua belah pihak terbunuh. Al-'Adid menyaksikan pertempuran dari istana, dan pasukan Syam diserang dengan batu dan panah yang datang dari arah istananya. Dikatakan bahwa hal ini terjadi atas perintah al-'Adid, dan ada yang mengatakan bukan demikian. Kemudian saudara Salahuddin, Syamsuddin Turanshah — yang datang untuk berperang dan dikirim oleh Noor ad-Deen untuk membantu dan mendukung saudaranya — memerintahkan agar balkon al-'Adid dibakar. Kemudian pintu dibuka dan sebuah suara berseru: Amirul Mu'minin memerintahkan kalian untuk mengusir orang-orang Sudan ini dari antara kalian dan dari tanah kalian. Semangat pasukan Syam pun menguat sementara tekad pasukan Sudan semakin melemah. Salahuddin mengirimkan pasukan ke tempat mereka yang dikenal sebagai al-Mansurah, di mana mereka memiliki rumah-rumah dan keluarga di dekat Bab Zuwailah, dan membakarnya. Pada saat itu mereka berbalik dan melarikan diri sementara pedang menghantam mereka dari segala arah, dan banyak dari mereka yang terbunuh. Kemudian mereka meminta perlindungan dari Salahuddin dan ia pun memberikannya kepada mereka, lalu mengasingkan mereka ke Giza. Kemudian Syamsuddin Turanshah, saudara Salahuddin, pergi menemui mereka dan membunuh sebagian besar dari mereka, hanya menyisakan sedikit dari mereka. Itulah rumah-rumah mereka yang kosong akibat kezaliman yang mereka lakukan (Al-Qur'an 27: 52). Tampaknya penguasa Mesir saat itu, al-'Adid, mengetahui konspirasi kepercayaan khalifah, karena mustahil membayangkan semua itu terjadi di istananya tanpa sepengetahuannya. Hal ini didukung oleh kenyataan bahwa ketika mereka berupaya menumpas para konspirator, pasukan Salahuddin diserang dengan batu dan panah yang datang dari arah istana penguasa, dan memang al-'Adid menyaksikan pertempuran dari istana. Pengungkapan konspirasi merupakan tanggung jawab departemen intelijen, dan secara khusus al-Qadi al-Fadil, yang merupakan otak di balik runtuhnya pengaruh Fathimiyyin di Mesir dan pembentukan mazhab Sunni. Kita akan membahasnya secara rinci insya Allah ketika membahas negara Ayyubiyah. Al-Qadi al-Fadil mengawasi para juru tulis departemen intelijen, terutama yang telah dipecat. Para mata-mata ada di mana-mana, di istana, di antara pasukan, di sepanjang perbatasan, di setiap pos di jalan, mengawasi komunikasi antara Mesir dan Tentara Salib. Mata-mata ini berhubungan langsung dengan al-Qadi al-Fadil, mengiriminya laporan melalui utusan dan merpati pos. Menghadapi orang-orang Armenia Namun kemenangan Salahuddin tidak berhenti dengan mengakhiri pengaruh pasukan Sudan; ia melanjutkan dengan mengakhiri pengaruh orang-orang Armenia juga. Mereka berada di urutan kedua setelah orang Sudan dalam kekuatan dan jumlah. Ia membakar rumah-rumah Armenia di antara dua istana, di mana terdapat sejumlah besar pasukan Armenia, sebagian besar merupakan pemanah yang mendapat gaji dari pemerintah. Mereka telah mencoba menghalangi pasukan Salahuddin selama pertempuran dengan orang Sudan dengan melepaskan anak panah, sehingga kini mereka mendapat balasan yang setimpal.
